[[sn:Seiryū Gongen]] [[ja:清瀧権現]]

[[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the head temple of the {{ill|Shingon|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are {{ill|Jundei Kannon|ja|准胝観音|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nyoirin Kannon|ja|如意輪観音|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a dragon king from {{ill|Indian mythology|ja|インド神話|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, {{ill|Zennyo Ryūō|ja|善女竜王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of {{ill|Seiryū-ji (China)|ja|青龍寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive {{ill|Samaya precepts|ja|三昧耶戒|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|qq=draft|qq=draft}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|qq=draft|qq=draft}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshō-ji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

[[sn:Seiryū Gongen]] [[ja:清瀧権現]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|右京区|" to "|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|"
revision.text:
[[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the head temple of the {{ill|Shingon|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are {{ill|Jundei Kannon|ja|准胝観音|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nyoirin Kannon|ja|如意輪観音|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a dragon king from {{ill|Indian mythology|ja|インド神話|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, {{ill|Zennyo Ryūō|ja|善女竜王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of {{ill|Seiryū-ji (China)|ja|青龍寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive {{ill|Samaya precepts|ja|三昧耶戒|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|qq=draft|qq=draft}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|qq=draft|qq=draft}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshō-ji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{shinto-stub}} {{密教2}} {{Buddhism2}} {{神道 横}} {{中世神道}}

It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshō-ji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

[[sn:Seiryū Gongen]] [[ja:清瀧権現]]

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
[[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo-ji|qq=}}, the head temple of the {{ill|Shingon|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon|qq=}} {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo school|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|qq=}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are {{ill|Jundei Kannon|ja|准胝観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jundei Kannon|qq=}} and {{ill|Nyoirin Kannon|ja|如意輪観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyoirin Kannon|qq=}}.
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}, she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|qq=}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|qq=}}, a dragon king from {{ill|Indian mythology|ja|インド神話|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian mythology|qq=}}. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|qq=}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, {{ill|Zennyo Ryūō|ja|善女竜王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zennyo Ryūō|qq=}} (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen-en|qq=}}.
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of {{ill|Seiryū-ji (China)|ja|青龍寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-ji (China)|qq=}}. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive {{ill|Samaya precepts|ja|三昧耶戒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya precepts|qq=}} but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|qq=}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|qq=}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|qq=}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|qq=}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|qq=}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|qq=}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|qq=}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|qq=}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|qq=}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|qq=}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|qq=}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}, built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo-ji|qq=}}. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|qq=}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshō-ji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshō-ji|qq=}} – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Bosatsu|qq=}}, the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|qq=}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōya|qq=}} – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|qq=}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|qq=}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|qq=}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|qq=}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}, built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|qq=}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshō-ji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshō-ji|qq=}} – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Bosatsu|qq=}}, the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|qq=}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōya|qq=}} – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|qq=}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|qq=}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|qq=}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|qq=}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

[[sn:Seiryū Gongen]] [[ja:清瀧権現]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Shingon Daigo School]]
revision.text:
[[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo-ji|qq=}}, the head temple of the {{ill|Shingon|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon|qq=}} {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo school|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|qq=}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are {{ill|Jundei Kannon|ja|准胝観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jundei Kannon|qq=}} and {{ill|Nyoirin Kannon|ja|如意輪観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyoirin Kannon|qq=}}.
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}, she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|qq=}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|qq=}}, a dragon king from {{ill|Indian mythology|ja|インド神話|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian mythology|qq=}}. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|qq=}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, {{ill|Zennyo Ryūō|ja|善女竜王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zennyo Ryūō|qq=}} (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen-en|qq=}}.
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of {{ill|Seiryū-ji (China)|ja|青龍寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-ji (China)|qq=}}. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive {{ill|Samaya precepts|ja|三昧耶戒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya precepts|qq=}} but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|qq=}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|qq=}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|qq=}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|qq=}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|qq=}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|qq=}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|qq=}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|qq=}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|qq=}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|qq=}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|qq=}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}, built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo-ji|qq=}}. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|qq=}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshō-ji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshō-ji|qq=}} – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Bosatsu|qq=}}, the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|qq=}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōya|qq=}} – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|qq=}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|qq=}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|qq=}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|qq=}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

[[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo-ji|qq=}}, the head temple of the {{ill|Shingon|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon|qq=}} {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo school|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|qq=}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are {{ill|Jundei Kannon|ja|准胝観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jundei Kannon|qq=}} and {{ill|Nyoirin Kannon|ja|如意輪観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyoirin Kannon|qq=}}.

As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|qq=}}.

From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|qq=}} preserved at Daigo-ji.

== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|qq=}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|qq=}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}, built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo-ji|qq=}}. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|qq=}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshō-ji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshō-ji|qq=}} – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Bosatsu|qq=}}, the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|qq=}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōya|qq=}} – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|qq=}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|qq=}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|qq=}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|qq=}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

[[sn:Seiryū Gongen]] [[ja:清瀧権現]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
[[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]], the head temple of the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo school|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]]. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|qq=}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are [[:en:Cundi (Buddhism)|Jundei Kannon]] and [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]].
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|qq=}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|qq=}}, a dragon king from [[:en:Hindu mythology|Indian mythology]]. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|qq=}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, [[:en:Zennyo Ryūō|Zennyo Ryūō]] (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]].
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of [[:en:Qinglong Temple|Seiryū-ji (China)]]. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive [[:en:Samaya|Samaya precepts]] but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|qq=}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|qq=}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|qq=}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|qq=}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|qq=}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|qq=}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|qq=}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|qq=}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|qq=}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|qq=}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|qq=}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|qq=}} – Located in [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]]. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|qq=}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshō-ji]] – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|qq=}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]] – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|qq=}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|qq=}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|qq=}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|qq=}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|qq=}} – Located in [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|qq=}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshō-ji]] – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|qq=}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]] – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|qq=}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. [[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]], the head temple of the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo school|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]]. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|qq=}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are [[:en:Cundi (Buddhism)|Jundei Kannon]] and [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]].
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|qq=}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|qq=}}, a dragon king from [[:en:Hindu mythology|Indian mythology]]. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|qq=}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, [[:en:Zennyo Ryūō|Zennyo Ryūō]] (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]].
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of [[:en:Qinglong Temple|Seiryū-ji (China)]]. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive [[:en:Samaya|Samaya precepts]] but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|qq=}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|qq=}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|qq=}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|qq=}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|qq=}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|qq=}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|qq=}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|qq=}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|qq=}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|qq=}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|qq=}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|qq=}} – Located in [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]]. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|qq=}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshō-ji]] – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|qq=}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]] – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|qq=}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|qq=}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|qq=}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|qq=}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|qq=}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|qq=}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|qq=}} – Located in [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]]. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|qq=}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshō-ji]] – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|qq=}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]] – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|qq=}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|qq=}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|qq=}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|qq=}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

[[sn:Seiryū Gongen]] [[ja:清瀧権現]] [[Category:Nāgas]] [[Category:天部]] [[Category:Japanese dragons]] [[Category:真言宗醍醐派]]

[[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]], the head temple of the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo school|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]]. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|qq=}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are [[:en:Cundi (Buddhism)|Jundei Kannon]] and [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]].
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|qq=}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|qq=}}, a dragon king from [[:en:Hindu mythology|Indian mythology]]. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|qq=}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, [[:en:Zennyo Ryūō|Zennyo Ryūō]] (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]].
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of [[:en:Qinglong Temple|Seiryū-ji (China)]]. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive [[:en:Samaya|Samaya precepts]] but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|qq=}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|qq=}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|qq=}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|qq=}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|qq=}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|qq=}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|qq=}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|qq=}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|qq=}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.

Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|qq=}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|qq=}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|qq=}}.

[[sn:Seiryū Gongen]] [[ja:清瀧権現]] [[Category:Nāgas]] [[Category:天部]] [[Category:Japanese dragons]] [[Category:真言宗醍醐派]] [[Category:Dharmapalas]] [[Category:Chinjusha]]

[[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]], the head temple of the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo school|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]]. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|qq=}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are [[:en:Cundi (Buddhism)|Jundei Kannon]] and [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]].
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|qq=}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|qq=}}, a dragon king from [[:en:Hindu mythology|Indian mythology]]. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|qq=}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, [[:en:Zennyo Ryūō|Zennyo Ryūō]] (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]].
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of [[:en:Qinglong Temple|Seiryū-ji (China)]]. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive [[:en:Samaya|Samaya precepts]] but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|qq=}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|qq=}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|qq=}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|qq=}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|qq=}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|qq=}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|qq=}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|qq=}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|qq=}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|qq=}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|qq=}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|qq=}} – Located in [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]]. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|qq=}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshō-ji]] – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|qq=}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]] – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|qq=}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|qq=}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|qq=}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|qq=}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|qq=}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|qq=}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|qq=}} – Located in [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]]. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|qq=}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|qq=}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshō-ji]] – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|qq=}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]] – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|qq=}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|qq=}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|qq=}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|qq=}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>
