revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] {{Infobox deity |associated_deities ={{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712}}; {{ill|Cundi|WD=Q3007546}}|attributes ={{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712}}; {{ill|Cundi|WD=Q3007546}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11561324]] Wikidata name: Seiryū Gongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]], the head temple of the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo school|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗醍醐派|1=Shingon-shū Daigo-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10437874}}, located in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]]. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清瀧権現|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are [[:en:Cundi (Buddhism)|Jundei Kannon]] and [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]].
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|1=Eight Great Dragon Kings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390736}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a dragon king from [[:en:Hindu mythology|Indian mythology]]. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, [[:en:Zennyo Ryūō|Zennyo Ryūō]] (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]].
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of [[:en:Qinglong Temple|Seiryū-ji (China)]]. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive [[:en:Samaya|Samaya precepts]] but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|1=Takao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673228}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|1=Kiyotaki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279513}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|1=Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451143}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|1=Eun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492432}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 密教|1=mikkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q336102}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|1=Shōkaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400488}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|1=Seiryu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228916}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located in [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]]. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平間寺|1=Kawasaki Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1504465}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshō-ji]] – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|1=Takao-san Yakuō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8194732}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]] – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|1=Kasatori Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599853}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|1=Kiyotaki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279513}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|1=Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451143}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|1=Eun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492432}}.

== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|1=Seiryu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228916}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located in [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]]. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平間寺|1=Kawasaki Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1504465}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshō-ji]] – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|1=Takao-san Yakuō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8194732}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]] – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|1=Kasatori Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599853}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] {{Infobox deity |associated_deities ={{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712}}; {{ill|Cundi|WD=Q3007546}}|attributes ={{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712}}; {{ill|Cundi|WD=Q3007546}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11561324]] Wikidata name: Seiryū Gongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]], the head temple of the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo school|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗醍醐派|1=Shingon-shū Daigo-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10437874}}, located in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]]. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清瀧権現|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are [[:en:Cundi (Buddhism)|Jundei Kannon]] and [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]].
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|1=Eight Great Dragon Kings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390736}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a dragon king from [[:en:Hindu mythology|Indian mythology]]. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, [[:en:Zennyo Ryūō|Zennyo Ryūō]] (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]].
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of [[:en:Qinglong Temple|Seiryū-ji (China)]]. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive [[:en:Samaya|Samaya precepts]] but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|1=Takao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673228}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|1=Kiyotaki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279513}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|1=Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451143}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|1=Eun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492432}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 密教|1=mikkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q336102}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|1=Shōkaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400488}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.

Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|1=Kasatori Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599853}}.

== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|1=Seiryu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228916}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located in [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]]. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平間寺|1=Kawasaki Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1504465}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshō-ji]] – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|1=Takao-san Yakuō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8194732}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]] – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|1=Kasatori Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599853}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

[[ja:清瀧権現]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:清瀧權現]] {{wikidata link|Q11561324}} [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Chinjusha (Guardian Shrines)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Deva (Buddhism)]] [[Category:Dharmapalas]] [[Category:Gongen]] [[Category:Shingon Daigo branch]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] {{Infobox deity |associated_deities ={{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712}}; {{ill|Cundi|WD=Q3007546}}|attributes ={{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712}}; {{ill|Cundi|WD=Q3007546}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11561324]] Wikidata name: Seiryū Gongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]], the head temple of the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo school|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗醍醐派|1=Shingon-shū Daigo-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10437874}}, located in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]]. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清瀧権現|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are [[:en:Cundi (Buddhism)|Jundei Kannon]] and [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]].
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|1=Eight Great Dragon Kings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390736}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a dragon king from [[:en:Hindu mythology|Indian mythology]]. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, [[:en:Zennyo Ryūō|Zennyo Ryūō]] (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]].
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of [[:en:Qinglong Temple|Seiryū-ji (China)]]. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive [[:en:Samaya|Samaya precepts]] but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|1=Takao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673228}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|1=Kiyotaki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279513}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|1=Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451143}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|1=Eun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492432}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 密教|1=mikkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q336102}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|1=Shōkaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400488}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|1=Seiryu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228916}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located in [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]]. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平間寺|1=Kawasaki Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1504465}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshō-ji]] – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|1=Takao-san Yakuō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8194732}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]] – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|1=Kasatori Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599853}}.

She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清瀧権現|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are {{ill|Cundi (Buddhism)|en|Cundi (Buddhism)|WD=Q3007546|lt=Jundei Kannon}} and {{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|en|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712|lt=Nyoirin Kannon}}.
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}}, she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|1=Eight Great Dragon Kings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390736}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a dragon king from {{ill|Hindu mythology|en|Hindu mythology|WD=Q878062|lt=Indian mythology}}. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, {{ill|Zennyo Ryūō|en|Zennyo Ryūō|WD=Q5931305|lt=Zennyo Ryūō}} (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|WD=Q11590468|lt=Shinsen-en}}.
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of {{ill|Qinglong Temple|en|Qinglong Temple|WD=Q11662838|lt=Seiryū-ji (China)}}. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive {{ill|Samaya|en|Samaya|WD=Q7408931|lt=Samaya precepts}} but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|1=Takao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673228}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|1=Kiyotaki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279513}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|1=Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451143}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|1=Eun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492432}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 密教|1=mikkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q336102}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|1=Shōkaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400488}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|1=Seiryu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228916}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji}}. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平間寺|1=Kawasaki Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1504465}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshō-ji}} – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}, the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|1=Takao-san Yakuō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8194732}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Mount Kōya}} – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|1=Kasatori Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599853}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] {{Infobox deity |associated_deities ={{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712}}; {{ill|Cundi|WD=Q3007546}}|attributes ={{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712}}; {{ill|Cundi|WD=Q3007546}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11561324]] Wikidata name: Seiryū Gongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of {{ill|Daigo-ji|en|Daigo-ji|WD=Q1157535|lt=Daigo-ji}}, the head temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon}} {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo school|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗醍醐派|1=Shingon-shū Daigo-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10437874}}, located in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|en|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1197771|lt=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清瀧権現|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are {{ill|Cundi (Buddhism)|en|Cundi (Buddhism)|WD=Q3007546|lt=Jundei Kannon}} and {{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|en|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712|lt=Nyoirin Kannon}}.
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}}, she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|1=Eight Great Dragon Kings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390736}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a dragon king from {{ill|Hindu mythology|en|Hindu mythology|WD=Q878062|lt=Indian mythology}}. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, {{ill|Zennyo Ryūō|en|Zennyo Ryūō|WD=Q5931305|lt=Zennyo Ryūō}} (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|WD=Q11590468|lt=Shinsen-en}}.
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of {{ill|Qinglong Temple|en|Qinglong Temple|WD=Q11662838|lt=Seiryū-ji (China)}}. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive {{ill|Samaya|en|Samaya|WD=Q7408931|lt=Samaya precepts}} but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|1=Takao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673228}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|1=Kiyotaki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279513}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|1=Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451143}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|1=Eun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492432}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 密教|1=mikkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q336102}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|1=Shōkaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400488}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|1=Seiryu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228916}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji}}. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平間寺|1=Kawasaki Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1504465}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshō-ji}} – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}, the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|1=Takao-san Yakuō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8194732}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Mount Kōya}} – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|1=Kasatori Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599853}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|WD=Q11590468|lt=Shinsen-en}}.

When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive {{ill|Samaya|en|Samaya|WD=Q7408931|lt=Samaya precepts}} but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|1=Takao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673228}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|1=Kiyotaki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279513}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|1=Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451143}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|1=Eun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492432}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 密教|1=mikkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q336102}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|1=Shōkaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400488}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|1=Seiryu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228916}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji}}. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平間寺|1=Kawasaki Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1504465}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshō-ji}} – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}, the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|1=Takao-san Yakuō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8194732}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Mount Kōya}} – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|1=Kasatori Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599853}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Seiryū Gongen]][[ja:清瀧権現]][[zh:清瀧權現]] {{Infobox deity |associated_deities ={{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712}}; {{ill|Cundi|WD=Q3007546}}|attributes ={{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712}}; {{ill|Cundi|WD=Q3007546}}}} [[File:KenponChoshokuSeiryuGongenZo.jpg|alt=Silk painting of Seiryū Gongen (Hatakeyama Memorial Museum) Kamakura period|thumb]] {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} (せいりゅうごんげん or せいりょうごんげん) is the guardian deity of {{ill|Daigo-ji|en|Daigo-ji|WD=Q1157535|lt=Daigo-ji}}, the head temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon}} {{ill|Daigo school|ja|醍醐派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo school|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗醍醐派|1=Shingon-shū Daigo-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10437874}}, located in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|en|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1197771|lt=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}. She is also called {{ill|Seiryū Daigongen|ja|清瀧大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清瀧権現|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}, and her name is sometimes written as 清滝権現. Her honji-butsu (original Buddhist forms) are {{ill|Cundi (Buddhism)|en|Cundi (Buddhism)|WD=Q3007546|lt=Jundei Kannon}} and {{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|en|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712|lt=Nyoirin Kannon}}.
== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}}, she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|1=Eight Great Dragon Kings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390736}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a dragon king from {{ill|Hindu mythology|en|Hindu mythology|WD=Q878062|lt=Indian mythology}}. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, {{ill|Zennyo Ryūō|en|Zennyo Ryūō|WD=Q5931305|lt=Zennyo Ryūō}} (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|WD=Q11590468|lt=Shinsen-en}}.
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of {{ill|Qinglong Temple|en|Qinglong Temple|WD=Q11662838|lt=Seiryū-ji (China)}}. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive {{ill|Samaya|en|Samaya|WD=Q7408931|lt=Samaya precepts}} but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|1=Takao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673228}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|1=Kiyotaki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279513}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|1=Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451143}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|1=Eun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492432}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 密教|1=mikkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q336102}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|1=Shōkaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400488}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|1=Seiryu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228916}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji}}. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平間寺|1=Kawasaki Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1504465}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshō-ji}} – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}, the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|1=Takao-san Yakuō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8194732}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Mount Kōya}} – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|1=Kasatori Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599853}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>

== History ==
Seiryū Gongen is not mentioned in Buddhist scriptures or tantric ritual texts. According to the teachings of {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}}, she is one of the {{ill|Eight Great Nāga Kings|ja|八大竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Nāga Kings|1=Eight Great Dragon Kings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390736}}, the third (or in some accounts, the fourth) princess of {{ill|Sāgara|ja|沙掲羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a dragon king from {{ill|Hindu mythology|en|Hindu mythology|WD=Q878062|lt=Indian mythology}}. She resided in {{ill|Anavatapta|ja|無熱地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While some dragons were considered malevolent, {{ill|Zennyo Ryūō|en|Zennyo Ryūō|WD=Q5931305|lt=Zennyo Ryūō}} (善如龍王) was a benevolent dragon, appearing as a golden serpent of 8 sun (approximately 2.5 cm), sitting atop a serpent of 9 shaku (just under 3 meters). She is said to have appeared when Kūkai performed a rain-invoking ritual at {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|WD=Q11590468|lt=Shinsen-en}}.
Zennyo Ryūō later flew to China, becoming the guardian deity of {{ill|Qinglong Temple|en|Qinglong Temple|WD=Q11662838|lt=Seiryū-ji (China)}}. When Kūkai visited Seiryū-ji to study Buddhism, he requested to receive {{ill|Samaya|en|Samaya|WD=Q7408931|lt=Samaya precepts}} but was refused. However, as he was returning to Japan, Zennyo Ryūō appeared aboard his ship, swearing to protect esoteric Buddhism. As a result, she was enshrined at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takao (Kyoto)|ja|高雄 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takao (Kyoto)|1=Takao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673228}}.
Since she had crossed the ocean, the "dragon" (龍) character was modified by adding the radical for water (氵), and she became known in Japan as {{ill|Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū (清瀧) Gongen|1=Seiryū Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561324}}. The river in Takao was also renamed {{ill|Kiyotaki River|ja|清滝川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotaki River|1=Kiyotaki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279513}}. She was later enshrined at {{ill|Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|安祥寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|1=Anshō-ji (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451143}} in Kyoto by {{ill|Eun|ja|恵運|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eun|1=Eun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492432}}.
After traveling across Japan, Seiryū Gongen descended to the summit of Daigo-ji around 900 CE (Shōtai 3), where she remained. From then on, she became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Shingon esoteric tradition|ja|真言密教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon esoteric tradition|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 密教|1=mikkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q336102}} preserved at Daigo-ji.
In 1097 CE (Jōtoku 1), {{ill|Shōkaku|ja|勝覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku|1=Shōkaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400488}} split the Daigo-ji shrine into an upper and lower precinct. Both sites have a main hall and a worship hall, but the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja|清瀧宮拝殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Worship Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Upper Daigo, built in the Muromachi period, is a National Treasure, while the {{ill|Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja|清瀧宮本殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-gū Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the foot of Daigo Mountain (Lower Daigo) is a National Important Cultural Property.
== Major Shrines and Temples Enshrining Seiryū Gongen ==
Seiryū Gongen is primarily enshrined at {{ill|Shingon temples|ja|真言宗の寺院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a protector of esoteric Buddhism. However, she was also worshipped at Shinto shrines until the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, after which some shrines replaced her with {{ill|Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} as the enshrined deity, as seen in {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)|1=Seiryu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228916}}.
* {{ill|Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja|青龍宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Shrine (青龍宮)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} style. According to a local information board, it was originally the guardian shrine of {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji}}. * {{ill|Upper Daigo-ji|ja|上醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Houses the Seiryū-gū Worship Hall and Main Hall. The worship hall is a National Treasure. * {{ill|Lower Daigo-ji|ja|下醍醐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Daigo-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 醍醐寺|1=Daigo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157535}} – Enshrined at the mountain’s base for easier access. It has both a worship hall and a main hall, with the main hall designated as an Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi|ja|川崎大師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Daishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平間寺|1=Kawasaki Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1504465}} – Enshrines Seiryū Gongen in a dedicated hall, said to have been brought from Daigo-ji. * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshō-ji}} – Seiryū Gongen is enshrined alongside {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}, the temple’s protective deity, in Seiryū Gongen Hall (built in 1732). * {{ill|Takao-san Yakuō-in|ja|高尾山薬王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Yakuō-in|1=Takao-san Yakuō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8194732}} – Features the Seiryū Hall at the Jakutaki ascetic training ground, enshrining Seiryū Daigongen. * {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Mount Kōya}} – In the {{ill|Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja|金剛峯寺壇上伽藍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōbu-ji Danjō Garan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Seiryū Daigongen Shrine stands beside the {{ill|Ajike Pond|ja|阿字池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajike Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|西笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, located on the eastern foothills of Daigo Mountain. Its "Seiryū Shrine Moon Viewing" is one of the {{ill|Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja|笠取八景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Eight Scenic Views|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja|東笠取清瀧宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikasatori Seiryū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}, located in the {{ill|Kasatori Mountains|ja|笠取山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasatori Mountains|1=Kasatori Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599853}}.
<Gallery> SeiryuGu Honden Kami-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Gongen Main Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward) Kamidaigo04 1024.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Upper Daigo-ji, National Treasure) Daigoji Kyoto04s5s4500.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Main Hall (Lower Daigo-ji, Important Cultural Property) SeiryuGu Haiden Shimo-Daigo.jpg|Seiryū Shrine Worship Hall (Lower Daigo-ji) 川崎大師清瀧権現堂01.JPG|Seiryū Gongen Hall (Kawasaki Daishi, Kanagawa Prefecture, Kawasaki City) </Gallery>
